description: Issue #188: CJK vertical writing mode support

userStories.title: Detect and handle WMode=1 (vertical writing)
userStories.description: CJK fonts can use vertical writing mode (WMode=1) where text advances vertically instead of horizontally. Detect WMode from CMap or font dictionary and apply vertical glyph metrics (W2/DW2) for text positioning.
acceptanceCriteria: WMode is detected from CMap streams and font dictionaries
Vertical writing mode uses W2/DW2 metrics for vertical advance
No regression on horizontal-mode CJK PDFs (cjk_mixed.pdf stays at F1=1.0)
userStories.notes: Key files: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (font setup and text positioning). WMode is found in: 1) CMap definition ('usecmap' or '/WMode' key), 2) Font dictionary /DescendantFonts CIDFont /CIDSystemInfo, 3) Predefined CMap name suffix (-V = vertical, -H = horizontal). W2 array format similar to W but with [cid [w1y v1x v1y ...]] for vertical metrics. DW2 default: [880 -1000]. Depends on #183 (CIDFont support) and #181 (per-char direction).
